---
title: "Anti-Piracy and Modular Licensing Solution for GProd"
subtitle: "A Secure, Flexible Hybrid Approach for Blazor Applications"
author: "Proposed Solution"
date: "January 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: serif
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: true
    logo: "https://via.placeholder.com/150x150?text=GProd"  # Replace with your logo URL if available
    footer: "GProd Licensing Solution"
---

## Overview

:::{.incremental}
- GProd is a **Blazor-based** product with two deployment models:
  - On-premise installation on client servers
  - Cloud-hosted (your infrastructure, client accesses via URL)
- Requirements:
  - Enforce **annual subscription** (disable on non-payment)
  - Prevent **illegal copies** and unauthorized installations
  - **Instantly enable/disable modules** (e.g., Resource Management) on subscription changes
:::

## Key Challenges

:::{.fragment}
- **Anti-Piracy**: Prevent copying/distributing on-premise installs
:::

:::{.fragment}
- **Subscription Enforcement**: Automatic expiry/disable after annual fee
:::

:::{.fragment}
- **Modular Control**: Real-time module toggling without redeploy/restart
:::

:::{.fragment}
- Support **both** on-premise (possible offline) and cloud scenarios
:::

## Recommended Hybrid Scheme

:::{.r-fit-text}
**Core Licensing** + **Feature Flags** = Secure + Instant Flexibility
:::

- **Licensing Layer**: Handles activation, subscription, hardware binding, anti-piracy
- **Feature Flag Layer**: Handles real-time module enabling/disabling

## Core Licensing: Aegis (.NET Open-Source)

:::{.incremental}
- Robust open-source .NET licensing library (GitHub: LSXPrime/Aegis)
- Supports:
  - Subscription licenses (annual expiry)
  - Node-locked (bind to hardware → prevents copies in on-premise)
  - Offline & online validation
  - Floating/concurrent if needed later
  - RSA encryption, signatures, tamper detection
- Includes optional **Aegis.Server** backend for centralized management
- Fluent API, easy Blazor integration
:::

## Why Aegis for Core Licensing?

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width="50%"}
**Pros**
- Fully .NET native
- Free & open-source
- Strong security features
- Supports on-premise hardware binding
- Subscription model built-in
:::

:::{.column width="50%"}
**Alternatives Considered**
- LicenseSpring (commercial, cloud-focused)
- Cryptlex / Cryptolens (popular cloud LaaS)
- Quick License Manager (QLM) (commercial, mature)
→ Aegis chosen for cost-free start + flexibility
:::
:::

## Feature Flags: FeatBit (Open-Source .NET-Focused)

:::{.incremental}
- Enterprise-grade open-source feature flag platform built in .NET
- Self-hostable (perfect for your control & data privacy)
- Real-time flag updates (WebSockets)
- .NET SDK for seamless Blazor integration
- Targeting rules, A/B testing, workflows
- Instant module toggling via dashboard/API
:::

## Why FeatBit for Modules?

:::{.columns}
:::{.column width="50%"}
**Advantages**
- Real-time changes (no app restart)
- Native .NET ecosystem
- Self-hosted → full control
- Advanced targeting & approvals
- Integrates well with Aegis checks
:::

:::{.column width="50%"}
**Other Options**
- Microsoft.FeatureManagement (lightweight, config-based)
- Unleash / Flagsmith (strong open-source)
- LaunchDarkly (commercial SaaS)
→ FeatBit best for .NET teams needing real-time + self-hosting
:::
:::

## Architecture Overview

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[Client GProd Blazor App] -->|Startup| B(Aegis License Validation)
    B -->|Hardware Bind + Subscription Check| C[Local License File]
    A -->|Periodic Heartbeat| D[Your License/Flag Server]
    D --> E(Aegis.Server: Validate/Track)
    D --> F(FeatBit Server: Feature Flags)
    A -->|Real-time Poll/WebSocket| F
    A -->|Module Visibility| G[Blazor Components Check Flags]

```

## Implementation Steps
:::{.incremental}

1. Set up central backend (ASP.NET API) hosting Aegis.Server + FeatBit
2. On payment/subscription update → Generate/update license + toggle flags
3. In GProd:
    - Startup: Aegis validates license (node-lock + expiry)
    - Runtime: Query FeatBit for module flags
    - Graceful fallback (offline grace period)
4. Add code obfuscation (.NET Reactor or Dotfuscator)
:::

How Instant Module Changes Work

- Client upgrades subscription → Your system toggles flag in FeatBit
- GProd app (on-premise or cloud) receives update via:
    - Polling (every 5-10 min)
    - WebSocket push (real-time)
- Blazor UI conditionally renders/enables module immediately

```C#
@if (await FeatureManager.IsEnabledAsync("ResourceManagement"))
{
    <ResourceManagementComponent />
}
```

## Anti-Piracy Protections
:::{.incremental}

- Node-locking to server hardware (MAC, CPU ID, fingerprint)
- Encrypted/signed license files
- Periodic online validation (heartbeat)
- Tamper detection & checksums
- Obfuscated code (prevent reverse-engineering)
- Central tracking of activations per customer
:::

## Benefits Summary
:::{.r-stack}
:::{.fragment}
Secure against casual & moderate piracy
:::
:::{.fragment}
Instant module control on subscription changes
:::
:::{.fragment}
Works offline with grace periods (on-premise friendly)
:::
:::{.fragment}
Low cost: Both core tools open-source/self-hosted
:::
:::{.fragment}
Scalable for future needs (concurrent, usage-based)
:::
:::
Next Steps
:::{.incremental}

- Prototype Aegis + FeatBit integration
- Test on-premise node-locking scenarios
- Simulate subscription upgrade → module enable
- Add obfuscation layer
- Roll out to beta clients for feedback
:::

Questions?
